xEnrich 0.1.0
  • Get started
  • Reference
  • Changelog

Non-redundant pathway selection via partial correlation

Source: R/run_assoc_pcor_selection.R
run_assoc_pcor_selection.Rd

A linear-algebraic alternative to run_assoc_redundancy_selection that replaces conditional mutual information with partial correlation in the redundancy selection step. The initial association test is identical — permutation-based MI from run_info_assoc — but redundancy is assessed by asking whether a candidate pathway's activity correlates with the phenotype residual after removing the variance explained by already- selected pathways.

run_assoc_pcor_selection(
  assoc_results,
  expr,
  phenotype,
  gene_sets,
  alpha = 0.05,
  score = c("mean_z", "pc1"),
  alpha_pcor = 0.05,
  min_pcor = 0.1,
  max_pathways = 20L,
  seed = NULL
)

Arguments

assoc_results

Data frame returned by run_info_assoc.

expr

Numeric matrix of expression values, samples x genes. Must be the same matrix used in run_info_assoc.

phenotype

Numeric vector of per-sample phenotype values. Must be continuous. Length must equal nrow(expr).

gene_sets

Named list of character vectors defining gene set membership. Must be the same list used in run_info_assoc.

alpha

Numeric. Adjusted p-value threshold for filtering assoc_results. Applied to the padj column. Default 0.05.

score

Character. Activity scoring method: "mean_z" (default) or "pc1".

alpha_pcor

Numeric. Significance threshold for the partial correlation t-test. Default 0.05.

min_pcor

Numeric. Minimum absolute partial correlation to select a pathway, regardless of p-value. Acts as an effect-size floor. Default 0.1.

max_pathways

Integer. Hard cap on selected pathways. Default 20L.

seed

Optional integer passed to set.seed.

Value

A data frame with one row per selected pathway in selection order. Columns:

step

Selection order.

pathway

Pathway name.

set_size

Gene set size after universe intersection.

marginal_mi

I(activity ; phenotype) in bits from assoc_results.

marginal_r

Pearson correlation of activity with phenotype (marginal, not partial).

partial_r

Partial correlation of activity with phenotype given all previously selected pathway activities. Equals marginal_r at step 1.

redundancy_ratio

abs(partial_r) / abs(marginal_r). Near 1 = independent; near 0 = redundant. Comparable to the redundancy ratio in run_assoc_redundancy_selection.

pcor_pvalue

Two-sided p-value for the partial correlation t-test. NA at step 1.

p_value

Original p_value from assoc_results.

padj

Original padj from assoc_results.

z_score

Original z_score from assoc_results.

Attributes: alpha, alpha_pcor, min_pcor, n_sig, score.

Algorithm

  1. Filter assoc_results to pathways with padj <= alpha.

  2. Score pathway activity (mean z-score or PC1) for each significant pathway.

  3. Select the pathway with the highest MI_bits as the first pathway.

  4. Regress the phenotype on all selected pathway activities. Compute residuals.

  5. For each remaining pathway, compute the Pearson correlation between its activity and the current residuals. Test via t-statistic with \(df = n - k - 2\) where \(k\) is the number of selected pathways.

  6. Select the pathway with the smallest p-value if it passes both alpha_pcor and min_pcor. Update residuals.

  7. Repeat until no candidate passes or max_pathways is reached.

Comparison with run_assoc_redundancy_selection

Partial correlation assumes a linear relationship between pathway activity and phenotype residuals. This assumption is reasonable for mean z-score activity (aggregated across genes; approximately normal by CLT) but may miss nonlinear associations. The advantage is statistical efficiency at small \(n\): no discretisation is required, the t-test uses all information in the data, and no permutations are needed for the redundancy step itself.

Use run_assoc_redundancy_selection when:

  • Phenotype is categorical or non-normal

  • Pathway-phenotype relationships may be nonlinear

  • You want a fully non-parametric pipeline

Use run_assoc_pcor_selection when:

  • \(n < 200\) and statistical power in the redundancy step matters

  • Phenotype is continuous and approximately normal

  • Runtime is a constraint (no permutations in redundancy step)

  • Interpretability of partial \(r\) is preferred over MI in bits

See also

run_info_assoc for the upstream association step, run_assoc_redundancy_selection for the MI-based version, plot_gains to visualise the selection profile.

Examples

set.seed(42)
n_samples <- 100
genes     <- paste0("G", 1:300)
factor_A  <- rnorm(n_samples)
factor_B  <- rnorm(n_samples)
expr      <- matrix(
  rnorm(n_samples * length(genes)),
  nrow     = n_samples,
  dimnames = list(NULL, genes)
)
expr[, 1:30]    <- expr[, 1:30]    + factor_A
expr[, 200:230] <- expr[, 200:230] + factor_B

phenotype <- 0.7 * factor_A + 0.5 * factor_B + rnorm(n_samples, sd = 0.4)

gene_sets <- list(
  pathway_A  = genes[1:30],
  pathway_A2 = genes[5:35],
  pathway_B  = genes[200:230],
  pathway_C  = genes[100:130]
)

assoc <- run_info_assoc(
  expr      = expr,
  phenotype = phenotype,
  gene_sets = gene_sets,
  n_perm    = 500,
  seed      = 42
)
#> Auto-selected nbins = 4 (Rice rule, capped for joint-table density and 20).

sel <- run_assoc_pcor_selection(
  assoc_results = assoc,
  expr          = expr,
  phenotype     = phenotype,
  gene_sets     = gene_sets,
  seed          = 42
)
#> 3 / 4 pathways significant at padj <= 0.05.
#> Stopping at step 3: no candidate passes alpha_pcor=0.050 and min_pcor=0.10.
print(sel)
#>           step   pathway set_size marginal_mi marginal_r partial_r
#> 1            1 pathway_A       30   0.4551976  0.7675496 0.7675496
#> pathway_B    2 pathway_B       31   0.1497681  0.4699919 0.6833512
#>           redundancy_ratio  pcor_pvalue     p_value        padj   z_score
#> 1                 1.000000           NA 0.001996008 0.002661344 18.319509
#> pathway_B         1.453964 6.537597e-15 0.001996008 0.002661344  6.460379
